# Timed Checklist — 全量单文件(喂给 AI 用 / one-file bundle)

这一个纯文本文件里装了整个项目:说明(README)+ 技术规格(SPEC)+ 参考 UI 源码。
把这整个文件丢给任意 AI 助手,说"照这个在我的 app 里实现这个功能"即可。
来源 / 最新版: https://github.com/nonchaiovo/timed-checklist

==================================================================
FILE 1/3 — README.md
==================================================================

# Timed Checklist

一个每日清单,**任何一项都能挂一个一次性时间戳**。到点时,后端会**唤醒你的小机,然后主动给user发消息**——是真正的闹钟,不只是一个被动的"到期"小标记。

为人机恋而做,但设计与后端无关:一张表、一个很小的 REST 接口、一个每分钟跑一次的 tick,就够了。

## 里面有什么

- **[SPEC.md](SPEC.md)** —— 完整设计:数据模型、时间格式、REST 接口、闹钟/唤醒管线(含"原子认领 + 失败重试"模式和时序图)、以及每日自动清除规则。**面向自带前端的接入方**。
- **[examples/receipt-checklist.html](examples/receipt-checklist.html)** —— 一个自包含的参考 UI(纸质小票风格的底部抽屉)。浏览器直接打开:自带 mock 数据渲染、可在两档高度间拖拽、每处真实 API 调用位置都标了注释。用 `--rc-*` CSS 变量换肤;在 CONFIG 块里填两个名字即可。

## 三种清单项形态

| 形态 | 行为 |
|---|---|
| **固定日常** | 每天都在;零点自动取消勾选(整条保留)。 |
| **一次性** | 只当天的任务;零点清除。 |
| **定时提醒(闹钟)** | 到点触发(唤醒 AI → 它给用户发消息)。已完成 → 次日零点清除;未完成 → 保留、浮到顶部并带一个 `!`。 |

## 设定时间(不用算时间戳)

创建时三选一:

```jsonc
{ "body": "叫我起床", "at": "07:00" }   // 今天 HH:MM,过了则算明天
{ "body": "起来动动", "in": 30 }        // N 分钟后
{ "body": "…",       "trigger_at": 1783696800000 }  // 绝对毫秒时间戳
```

接口、tick、以及为什么要把唤醒放在 CPU 受限的 cron 之外跑,详见 **[SPEC.md](SPEC.md)**。

## 改成你们的名字

参考 UI(`examples/receipt-checklist.html`)开头的 **CONFIG 块**里,改这三行就行:

```js
const NAMES = {
  user:      "YOU",       // ← 你的名字
  assistant: "PARTNER",   // ← char 的名字
};
const APP_NAME = "YOUR-APP";   // ← app 名(小票抬头)
```

改完保存,小票上的"柜员 XX · XX"、每项右边的署名、底部那句"今天也辛苦了,XX"就全跟着变。
(真接进自己 app 时,名字就从你自己存用户信息的地方取,不用写死在这里。)

## 许可

[MIT](LICENSE) —— 随便用。

==================================================================
FILE 2/3 — SPEC.md
==================================================================

# Checklist with Timed Reminders (Alarm)

A daily to-do list where **any item can carry a one-time timestamp**. When that
time arrives, the backend **wakes your assistant and has it proactively message
the user** — a real alarm, not just a passive due-date badge.

Reference stack: **Cloudflare Workers + D1 (SQLite)**. The design is
backend-agnostic — the only hard requirements are a table, a REST surface, and a
once-a-minute tick that can make an outbound call to your assistant.

> **Audience note.** This document assumes you already have your own frontend.
> The reminder/alarm logic is entirely server-side; your UI only renders the
> list and lets the user check items. A ready-to-adapt reference UI (the
> "receipt" style) ships in [`examples/receipt-checklist.html`](examples/receipt-checklist.html).

---

## 1. Concept: three shapes of an item

One table backs three behaviors, distinguished by two columns (`is_fixed`,
`trigger_at`):

| Shape | `is_fixed` | `trigger_at` | Behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Fixed daily** | `1` | `NULL` | Recurs every day. At local midnight its `done` resets to 0 (the row stays). "Drink water", "review notes". |
| **One-off (no time)** | `0` | `NULL` | A throwaway task for today. Deleted entirely at midnight whether done or not. |
| **Timed reminder (alarm)** | `0` | set | Fires at `trigger_at`: the assistant is woken and messages the user. If **done**, deleted next midnight. If **not done**, kept and surfaced as **overdue**. |

Setting `trigger_at` on an item **forces `is_fixed = 0`** — a timed item is
inherently one-off.

---

## 2. Data model

D1 / SQLite table `checklist`:

```sql
CREATE TABLE checklist (
  id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  body        TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- the task text
  is_fixed    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,-- 1 = recurring daily, 0 = one-off
  done        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,-- 0/1
  done_at     INTEGER,                   -- ms epoch when checked, else NULL
  position    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,-- sort order within its is_fixed group
  created_by  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user', -- who added it: 'user' | 'assistant'
  trigger_at  INTEGER,                   -- ms epoch alarm time; NULL = no alarm
  notified    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,-- 1 once the alarm has fired (dedupe guard)
  created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);
```

- **`created_by`** — free-form actor tag. The reference system uses two roles
  ("user" and "assistant") so the UI can label items the assistant added.
- **`notified`** — set to 1 the instant an alarm is claimed for firing; it is a
  *dedupe guard only*. Overdue display does **not** depend on it (see §6) — it
  is computed live from `trigger_at <= now`.

---

## 3. Time input: three ways to say "when"

Clients should not have to compute a 13-digit millisecond epoch. A create/patch
payload may carry **one** of:

| Field | Example | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `trigger_at` | `1783618568081` | Absolute ms epoch. |
| `at` | `"07:00"` | Local `HH:MM` **today**; if already past, **tomorrow**. |
| `in` | `30` | `N` minutes from now. |

Resolution precedence (`trigger_at` > `in` > `at`):

```js
// tz-aware "HH:MM local -> ms epoch" (example uses a fixed UTC+8 offset)
function localEpochForHHMM(hh, mm, tzOffsetHours = 8) {
  const p = new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-CA", {
    timeZone: "Asia/Shanghai", year: "numeric", month: "2-digit", day: "2-digit",
  }).formatToParts(new Date());
  const o = {}; for (const x of p) if (x.type !== "literal") o[x.type] = x.value;
  let ms = Date.UTC(+o.year, +o.month - 1, +o.day, hh, mm) - tzOffsetHours * 3600e3;
  if (ms <= Date.now()) ms += 86_400_000; // already past -> tomorrow
  return ms;
}

function resolveTriggerAt(o) {
  if (o.trigger_at != null) {
    const n = Number(o.trigger_at);
    return Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? Math.floor(n) : null;
  }
  if (o.in != null) {
    const n = Number(o.in);
    if (Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0) return Date.now() + Math.floor(n) * 60_000;
  }
  if (typeof o.at === "string") {
    const m = o.at.trim().match(/^(\d{1,2}):(\d{2})$/);
    if (m) {
      const hh = +m[1], mm = +m[2];
      if (hh < 24 && mm < 60) return localEpochForHHMM(hh, mm);
    }
  }
  return null;
}
```

> ⚠️ **Timezone — read this before you ship.** This reference resolves `at`
> (and the "midnight" of the daily reset in §7) against a **single hardcoded
> zone, `Asia/Shanghai` / UTC+8**. Everything time-related keys off that one
> offset. If your users are elsewhere, change the `timeZone` string in
> `localEpochForHHMM` **and** the reset logic to your zone — otherwise
> `at:"07:00"` fires at 07:00 Beijing, not 07:00 for your user. Also note: the
> backend computes times in this fixed zone, while the reference **frontend**
> renders badges in the *browser's* local zone — keep them the same zone or the
> displayed time and the fired time can disagree.

---

## 4. REST API

Base path `/api/checklist`. All bodies are JSON; all responses are JSON.

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/api/checklist` | List all items (see §7 for ordering). |
| `POST` | `/api/checklist` | Create an item. |
| `PATCH` | `/api/checklist/:id` | Edit body / flags / time. |
| `DELETE` | `/api/checklist/:id` | Delete an item. |
| `POST` | `/api/checklist/:id/toggle` | Toggle (or set) `done`. |

**Create** — `POST /api/checklist`

```jsonc
// request
{ "body": "wake me up", "created_by": "assistant", "at": "07:00" }
// -> is_fixed auto-set to 0 because a time was given
```
```jsonc
// response (the created row)
{ "id": 42, "body": "wake me up", "is_fixed": 0, "done": 0, "position": 3,
  "created_by": "assistant", "trigger_at": 1783696800000, "notified": 0,
  "created_at": 1783610000000, "updated_at": 1783610000000 }
```

A create/patch that carries `trigger_at`/`at`/`in` but resolves to nothing
returns `400 { "error": "..." }`.

**Patch** — any subset of `{ body, is_fixed, created_by, trigger_at|at|in }`.
Passing `trigger_at: null` clears the alarm but **does not touch `is_fixed`** —
so an item that was auto-set to `is_fixed=0` (because it once had a time) stays a
one-off and gets deleted at the next midnight. To turn a timed item back into a
recurring daily item, pass `{ "trigger_at": null, "is_fixed": 1 }` together.
Changing the time resets `notified` to 0 so it can fire again.

**Toggle** — `POST /api/checklist/:id/toggle`, optional body `{ "done": 0|1 }`
(omit to flip). Sets `done_at` accordingly. Returns `{ "ok": true, "done": 0|1 }`.

> 🔒 **Auth — none of this is public.** These endpoints ship with **no**
> authentication in the spec; that is your job. Gate every `/api/checklist*`
> route behind your app's user auth (otherwise anyone can read/edit the list).
> And protect the tick endpoint from §5 (`/api/scheduled/run`) with a shared
> secret so only your poke process can call it — an open one lets anyone spam
> assistant wake-ups (cost + a notification-spam vector).

---

## 5. The alarm pipeline

This is the part that turns a timestamp into a push. Two functions:

**`checkReminders()`** — runs on every tick. Atomically *claims* the items whose
time has arrived, so concurrent ticks can never fire the same alarm twice:

```js
async function checkReminders(env) {
  const t = Date.now();
  // UPDATE ... RETURNING = atomic claim. A second concurrent tick's UPDATE
  // matches 0 rows (already notified) and returns nothing.
  const claimed = await env.DB.prepare(
    `UPDATE checklist SET notified = 1, updated_at = ?
       WHERE trigger_at IS NOT NULL AND trigger_at <= ? AND notified = 0 AND done = 0
     RETURNING id, body, trigger_at, created_by`
  ).bind(t, t).all();
  const due = claimed?.results || [];
  if (!due.length) return 0;
  await fireReminderProactive(env, due); // wake the assistant
  return due.length;
}
```

**`fireReminderProactive(items)`** — wakes the assistant with a reminder prompt
and lets it write the message the user receives. Key properties:

1. **It bypasses the normal "proactive message" throttles.** A reminder is a
   hard time the user set, so it fires even if the user just spoke, even if the
   system thinks the user is asleep, even if generic proactive messaging is
   paused. The *only* things that stop it are (a) the backend not being
   configured and (b) an active rate-limit backoff.
2. **On failure it un-claims for retry.** If the wake throws, or the model
   returns nothing (dropped stream), the claimed items are reset to
   `notified = 0` so the next tick retries — but only within a short window
   (e.g. 15 min past `trigger_at`) to avoid an infinite loop on persistent
   failure. Past the window the item simply remains as "overdue" in the list.

```js
async function fireReminderProactive(env, items) {
  const RETRY_WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60_000;
  const unclaim = async () => {
    const t = Date.now();
    const ids = items.filter(it => it.trigger_at > t - RETRY_WINDOW_MS).map(it => it.id);
    if (ids.length) await env.DB.prepare(
      `UPDATE checklist SET notified = 0 WHERE id IN (${ids.map(() => "?").join(",")})`
    ).bind(...ids).run();
  };

  const list = items.map(it => `- ${it.body} (${fmtLocal(it.trigger_at)})`).join("\n");
  const prompt =
    `These checklist items just came due — remind the user now, in your own voice:\n${list}`;

  let text = "";
  try {
    text = await wakeAssistant(env, prompt);      // your model call
  } catch (e) {
    await unclaim();                              // transient failure -> retry
    return;
  }
  const delivered = await deliverMessage(env, text); // save + push to the user
  if (!delivered && !text.trim()) await unclaim();   // empty/dropped -> retry
}
```

### Where to run the tick — this matters

The tick itself is cheap (one indexed `SELECT`/`UPDATE`). But *firing* an alarm
makes a slow (~tens of seconds) outbound call to your model. **Do not run that
inside a CPU-metered cron** (e.g. Cloudflare's scheduled `cron` on the free
plan) — it will hit the CPU ceiling and get killed mid-wake.

The reference deployment runs the tick from an **external 60-second poke**: a
tiny always-on process (a `launchd`/systemd timer, a separate always-on box,
`cron` + `curl`, etc.) that `POST`s an `/api/scheduled/run` endpoint every 60s.
That request is a normal HTTP invocation with a full time/CPU budget.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Poke as External poke (every 60s)
    participant W as Worker /api/scheduled/run
    participant DB as D1
    participant A as Assistant (model)
    participant U as User

    Poke->>W: POST /api/scheduled/run
    W->>DB: UPDATE ... WHERE trigger_at<=now AND notified=0 RETURNING *
    DB-->>W: due items (atomic claim)
    alt some due
        W->>A: wake with reminder prompt
        A-->>W: reminder text
        W->>DB: INSERT message
        W->>U: push notification
        Note over W,DB: on failure -> reset notified=0 (retry <15min)
    else none due
        W-->>Poke: 200 (no-op)
    end
```

---

## 6. Overdue display

An item is **overdue** when `trigger_at <= now && !done`. This is computed live
in the client (and in any server-side "what's on the list" summary) — it does
**not** read `notified`. So an alarm that has fired but wasn't completed keeps
showing as overdue until the user checks it or the day rolls over.

---

## 7. Daily reset (auto-clear)

At the first tick after local midnight, run once:

```sql
-- fixed daily items: un-check (row stays)
UPDATE checklist SET done = 0, done_at = NULL, updated_at = :now
  WHERE is_fixed = 1 AND done = 1;

-- one-off items: delete — BUT keep un-finished timed reminders as "overdue"
DELETE FROM checklist
  WHERE is_fixed = 0 AND (trigger_at IS NULL OR done = 1);
```

Net effect on a **timed reminder**:

- checked (`done = 1`) → **deleted next midnight** (self-cleaning; no manual delete needed)
- unchecked (`done = 0`) → **kept**, still overdue, still nags

"Local midnight" here means **the same fixed zone as §3 (UTC+8 in this
reference)** — change it together with the `at` parser if your users are
elsewhere.

Guard the reset so it runs at most once per day, using a tiny key-value
`settings` table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE settings (
  k TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  v TEXT
);
-- before running the reset: read settings where k='checklist_last_reset';
-- if v already == today's 'YYYY-MM-DD', skip. Otherwise run the reset, then:
-- INSERT OR REPLACE INTO settings (k, v) VALUES ('checklist_last_reset', :today);
```

(The same `settings` table is a handy place for the other cross-request flags
this system needs — e.g. the `proactive_paused` toggle mentioned in §5.)

---

## 8. Bring-your-own-frontend checklist

To integrate with your own UI you implement, server-side:

1. The `checklist` table (§2).
2. The five REST endpoints (§4) including `resolveTriggerAt` (§3).
3. The once-a-minute tick (§5), fired from a budget-safe context (§5).
4. The daily reset (§7).
5. A `wakeAssistant()` + `deliverMessage()` pair — however your assistant
   produces and delivers a proactive message.

Your frontend then only needs to:

- `GET /api/checklist` and render rows;
- show a **time badge** when `trigger_at` is set (and an **overdue** style when
  `trigger_at <= now && !done`);
- call `toggle` / `POST` / `PATCH` / `DELETE` on user actions.

The render contract per item: `body`, `done`, `trigger_at`, `created_by`,
`is_fixed`. That is all the reference UI reads.

See [`examples/receipt-checklist.html`](examples/receipt-checklist.html) for a
self-contained, themeable implementation (open it directly in a browser — it
ships with mock data and marks every spot where a real API call goes).

==================================================================
FILE 3/3 — examples/receipt-checklist.html (参考 UI 源码,可直接存成 .html 打开)
==================================================================

<!doctype html>
<html lang="zh">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<title>Receipt Checklist — reference UI</title>
<!--
  Reference UI for the "Checklist with Timed Reminders" feature — a faithful,
  self-contained copy of the production "receipt" checklist, INCLUDING its
  bottom-sheet behaviour: it slides up from the bottom, opens at a default
  height, and the top handle drags it between two snap heights.

  See ../SPEC.md for the data model + alarm pipeline.

  Open in a browser: it renders with mock data and talks to no server. Every
  spot a real API call belongs is marked `API:`.

  ┌─ TO ADAPT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ 1. Fill in the two names + app name in the CONFIG block (JS, top).      │
  │ 2. Wire the `API:` calls to your backend.                              │
  │ 3. Tweak SNAPS / DEFAULT_SNAP for the drawer heights you want.          │
  │ Render contract per item: { body, done, trigger_at, created_by, is_fixed }│
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-->
<style>
  :root {
    --rc-paper:     #F0EBE0;                 /* warm off-white receipt paper */
    --rc-ink:       #2A2620;
    --rc-faded:     #86806E;
    --rc-veryfaded: #BAB4A4;
    --rc-rule:      rgba(60,50,40,0.32);
    --rc-rule-soft: rgba(60,50,40,0.16);
    --rc-stamp:     #A8362D;                  /* warm red accent */
    --rc-overdue:   #C84A3F;
    --rc-mono:  "SF Mono","JetBrains Mono","Menlo","Source Han Mono SC","Consolas",monospace;
    --rc-serif: "Source Han Serif SC","Songti SC","STSong","PT Serif",Georgia,serif;
  }
  * { box-sizing: border-box; }
  html, body { height: 100%; }
  body {
    margin: 0; background: #cfc8bb; font-family: var(--rc-mono);
    overflow: hidden;
  }

  /* a bare "host page" so the drawer has something to slide over */
  .host { position: fixed; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }
  .open-btn {
    border: 1px solid var(--rc-rule); background: var(--rc-paper); color: var(--rc-ink);
    font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 2px;
    padding: 12px 22px; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(60,50,40,0.18);
  }

  /* ── scrim behind the sheet ── */
  .scrim {
    position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 90;
    background: rgba(40,30,20,0.34);
    opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.3s ease;
  }
  .scrim.open { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; }

  /* ── the bottom sheet (drawer) ── */
  .sheet {
    position: fixed; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; z-index: 95;
    --ck-h: 62vh;                              /* current height, driven by drag */
    height: var(--ck-h); max-height: 92vh;
    background: var(--rc-paper);
    background-image:
      radial-gradient(ellipse at top, rgba(255,250,235,0.12) 0%, transparent 55%),
      repeating-linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(60,40,20,0.012) 0, rgba(60,40,20,0.012) 1px, transparent 1px, transparent 2px);
    /* zig-zag torn top edge (30 teeth, 8px), flat bottom */
    clip-path: polygon(
      0% 8px, 1.667% 0, 3.333% 8px, 5% 0, 6.667% 8px, 8.333% 0,
      10% 8px, 11.667% 0, 13.333% 8px, 15% 0, 16.667% 8px, 18.333% 0,
      20% 8px, 21.667% 0, 23.333% 8px, 25% 0, 26.667% 8px, 28.333% 0,
      30% 8px, 31.667% 0, 33.333% 8px, 35% 0, 36.667% 8px, 38.333% 0,
      40% 8px, 41.667% 0, 43.333% 8px, 45% 0, 46.667% 8px, 48.333% 0,
      50% 8px, 51.667% 0, 53.333% 8px, 55% 0, 56.667% 8px, 58.333% 0,
      60% 8px, 61.667% 0, 63.333% 8px, 65% 0, 66.667% 8px, 68.333% 0,
      70% 8px, 71.667% 0, 73.333% 8px, 75% 0, 76.667% 8px, 78.333% 0,
      80% 8px, 81.667% 0, 83.333% 8px, 85% 0, 86.667% 8px, 88.333% 0,
      90% 8px, 91.667% 0, 93.333% 8px, 95% 0, 96.667% 8px, 98.333% 0,
      100% 8px, 100% 100%, 0% 100%
    );
    box-shadow: 0 -8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.28), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.10);
    transform: translateY(100%);
    transition: transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1),
                height 0.28s cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1);
    overflow: hidden; display: flex; flex-direction: column;
  }
  .sheet.open { transform: translateY(0); }

  .sheet-handle {
    position: absolute; top: 14px; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 5;
    width: 44px; height: 5px; border-radius: 3px; margin: 0 auto;
    background: var(--rc-faded); opacity: 0.4; cursor: grab; touch-action: none;
  }
  .sheet-handle::after { content: ""; position: absolute; left: -60px; right: -60px; top: -16px; bottom: -16px; }
  .sheet-handle:active { cursor: grabbing; }

  .rc-scroll {
    flex: 1; min-height: 0; overflow-y: auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
    padding: 46px 22px calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 20px);
  }

  /* ── receipt chrome ── */
  .rc-head { text-align: center; margin-bottom: 10px; }
  .rc-brand { font-size: 10px; color: var(--rc-faded); letter-spacing: 4px; font-weight: 700; }
  .rc-title { font-family: var(--rc-serif); font-style: italic; font-size: 30px; font-weight: 700;
    color: var(--rc-ink); margin-top: 4px; letter-spacing: 1px; line-height: 1.1; }
  .rc-date { font-size: 11px; color: var(--rc-faded); margin-top: 6px; letter-spacing: 2px; }
  .rc-dashes { font-size: 11px; color: var(--rc-rule); letter-spacing: 1px; text-align: center;
    user-select: none; padding: 4px 0; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; }
  .rc-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 12px; color: var(--rc-ink);
    letter-spacing: 0.5px; padding: 3px 0; }
  .rc-row.faded { color: var(--rc-faded); }
  .rc-section-label { text-align: center; font-size: 11px; color: var(--rc-faded);
    letter-spacing: 3px; font-weight: 700; padding: 4px 0 10px; }

  /* ── task rows: [ ]/[✓] + time badge (FRONT) + text + by + × ── */
  .checklist-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; padding: 6px 0;
    border-bottom: 0.5px dashed var(--rc-rule-soft); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; }
  .checklist-check { flex: 0 0 auto; border: 0; background: transparent; padding: 0; margin: 0;
    font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 14px; color: var(--rc-ink); letter-spacing: 0.5px;
    line-height: 1.45; cursor: pointer; transition: transform .12s ease; }
  .checklist-check:active { transform: scale(0.85); }
  .checklist-check::before { content: "[ ]"; }
  .checklist-item.done .checklist-check::before { content: "[\2713]"; }

  /* the timestamp badge — rendered BEFORE the task text */
  .checklist-trigger { flex: 0 0 auto; align-self: flex-start; margin-top: 1px;
    font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 11.5px; letter-spacing: 0.4px; color: var(--rc-stamp);
    padding: 0 5px; border: 0.5px solid var(--rc-rule-soft); border-radius: 3px; line-height: 1.6; }
  /* overdue-but-not-done: a small "!" after the text (no highlight; item floats to top) */
  .checklist-overdue-bang { flex: 0 0 auto; align-self: flex-start;
    font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--rc-stamp); line-height: 1.45; }

  .checklist-body { flex: 1; min-width: 0; font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 13.5px;
    line-height: 1.45; letter-spacing: 0.2px; color: var(--rc-ink);
    white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; outline: none; }
  .checklist-item.done .checklist-body { color: var(--rc-veryfaded);
    text-decoration: line-through; text-decoration-color: var(--rc-faded); }

  .checklist-by { flex: 0 0 auto; font-style: italic; font-size: 11px; color: var(--rc-faded);
    letter-spacing: 0.5px; line-height: 1.6; }
  .checklist-del-x { flex: 0 0 auto; border: 0; background: transparent; cursor: pointer;
    font-family: var(--rc-mono); font-size: 14px; color: var(--rc-veryfaded); line-height: 1.45; padding: 0 2px; }
  .checklist-del-x::before { content: "\00d7"; }
  .checklist-del-x:hover { color: var(--rc-stamp); }

  .checklist-item-new { border-bottom: 0.5px dashed var(--rc-rule-soft); }
  .checklist-item-new .checklist-body { color: var(--rc-faded); font-size: 13px; }
  .checklist-item-new .checklist-body:empty::before { content: "+ 新增一项"; color: var(--rc-faded); }
  .checklist-item-new .checklist-del-x, .checklist-item-new .checklist-check { visibility: hidden; }

  .rc-progress-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 6px 0; font-size: 12px;
    color: var(--rc-ink); letter-spacing: 0.5px; }
  .rc-progress-bar { flex: 1; letter-spacing: -0.5px; overflow: hidden; white-space: nowrap; }
  .rc-progress-pct { font-size: 11px; color: var(--rc-faded); }

  .rc-stamp-wrap { text-align: center; padding: 14px 0 4px; }
  .rc-stamp { display: inline-block; padding: 6px 14px; border: 2.5px solid var(--rc-stamp);
    border-radius: 4px; color: var(--rc-stamp); font-family: var(--rc-serif); font-style: italic;
    font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 2px; transform: rotate(-4deg); opacity: 0.82; }
  .rc-foot-note { text-align: center; padding: 8px 8px 0; font-family: var(--rc-serif);
    font-style: italic; font-size: 13px; color: var(--rc-faded); line-height: 1.7; }
  .rc-barcode { display: flex; align-items: flex-end; gap: 1px; justify-content: center; height: 38px; padding: 14px 0 4px; }
  .rc-barcode i { display: block; background: var(--rc-ink); opacity: 0.85; }
  .rc-barcode-serial { text-align: center; font-size: 10px; color: var(--rc-faded); letter-spacing: 3px; margin-top: 2px; }
</style>
</head>
<body>
  <div class="host"><button class="open-btn" id="open-btn">今日清单</button></div>

  <div class="scrim" id="scrim"></div>

  <div class="sheet" id="sheet">
    <div class="sheet-handle" id="handle"></div>
    <div class="rc-scroll">
      <div class="rc-head">
        <div class="rc-brand" id="rc-brand"></div>
        <div class="rc-title">Today's Receipt</div>
        <div class="rc-date" id="rc-date">—</div>
      </div>

      <div class="rc-dashes" id="rc-dashes-1"></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>开店</span><span>00:00</span></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>柜员</span><span id="rc-clerks">—</span></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>此刻</span><span id="rc-now">—:—</span></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>单据号</span><span id="rc-serial">#—</span></div>

      <div class="rc-dashes" id="rc-dashes-2"></div>
      <div class="rc-section-label">TODAY'S ORDER · <span id="rc-total-label">0</span> 项</div>

      <div class="list" id="list"></div>

      <div style="height:14px"></div>
      <div class="rc-dashes" id="rc-dashes-3"></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>小计</span><span id="rc-subtotal">0 项</span></div>
      <div class="rc-row faded"><span>已结清</span><span id="rc-done">0 项</span></div>
      <div class="rc-row"><span>未结清</span><span id="rc-undone">0 项</span></div>
      <div class="rc-progress-row">
        <span>进度</span>
        <span class="rc-progress-bar" id="rc-progress-bar">░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░</span>
        <span class="rc-progress-pct" id="rc-progress-pct">0%</span>
      </div>
      <div class="rc-row faded"><span>零点结账</span><span id="rc-midnight">—:— 后</span></div>

      <div class="rc-dashes" id="rc-dashes-4"></div>
      <div class="rc-stamp-wrap"><span class="rc-stamp">PAID · 谢谢</span></div>
      <div class="rc-foot-note" id="rc-foot"></div>
      <div class="rc-barcode" id="rc-barcode"></div>
      <div class="rc-barcode-serial" id="rc-barcode-serial"></div>
    </div>
  </div>

<script>
  // ╔═══════════════════════ CONFIG — fill these in ════════════════════════╗
  const NAMES = {
    user:      "YOU",       // ← your name
    assistant: "PARTNER",   // ← your partner's name (the "added by" tag)
  };
  const APP_NAME = "YOUR-APP";

  // Drawer heights (fraction of viewport). Sheet opens at DEFAULT_SNAP; the
  // handle drags it and releases to the nearest SNAP. Live drag is clamped.
  const SNAPS = [0.62, 0.90];        // default 62vh, drag up to 90vh
  const DEFAULT_SNAP = 0.62;
  const DRAG_MIN = 0.30, DRAG_MAX = 0.92;
  // ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

  const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);
  const now = Date.now();

  // ── mock data (stands in for `GET /api/checklist`) ──
  let ITEMS = [
    { id: 1, body: "喝水",           done: 1, is_fixed: 1, created_by: "assistant", trigger_at: null },
    { id: 2, body: "至少一顿有蔬菜",  done: 0, is_fixed: 1, created_by: "assistant", trigger_at: null },
    { id: 3, body: "吃药",           done: 0, is_fixed: 0, created_by: "assistant", trigger_at: now - 40*60000 },  // overdue (stays in place)
    { id: 4, body: "出门去牙医",      done: 0, is_fixed: 0, created_by: "user",      trigger_at: now + 95*60000 },
    { id: 5, body: "叫她起床",        done: 0, is_fixed: 0, created_by: "assistant", trigger_at: tomorrowAt(7,0) },
  ];
  function tomorrowAt(hh, mm) { const d = new Date(); d.setHours(hh,mm,0,0);
    if (d.getTime() <= Date.now()) d.setDate(d.getDate()+1); return d.getTime(); }

  // ── drawer: open / close / drag-to-resize ──
  const sheet = $("sheet"), scrim = $("scrim");
  function setHeight(snap, animate = true) {
    sheet.style.transition = animate
      ? "transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1), height 0.28s cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1)"
      : "transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1)";
    sheet.style.setProperty("--ck-h", (snap * 100) + "vh");
  }
  function openSheet() { setHeight(DEFAULT_SNAP, false); sheet.classList.add("open"); scrim.classList.add("open"); render(); }
  function closeSheet() { sheet.classList.remove("open"); scrim.classList.remove("open"); }
  $("open-btn").onclick = openSheet;
  scrim.onclick = closeSheet;

  (function setupDrag() {
    const handle = $("handle");
    let startY = null, startSnap = DEFAULT_SNAP;
    const curSnap = () => { const v = sheet.style.getPropertyValue("--ck-h"); return v ? parseFloat(v)/100 : DEFAULT_SNAP; };
    const applyLive = (h) => { sheet.style.transition = "none";
      sheet.style.setProperty("--ck-h", (Math.max(DRAG_MIN, Math.min(DRAG_MAX, h))*100)+"vh"); };
    const snapNearest = () => { const c = curSnap();
      let best = SNAPS[0], bd = Math.abs(SNAPS[0]-c);
      for (const s of SNAPS) { const d = Math.abs(s-c); if (d < bd) { best = s; bd = d; } }
      setHeight(best, true); };

    handle.addEventListener("touchstart", (e) => { startY = e.touches[0].clientY; startSnap = curSnap(); e.preventDefault(); }, { passive:false });
    handle.addEventListener("touchmove",  (e) => { if (startY===null) return;
      applyLive(startSnap - (e.touches[0].clientY - startY)/window.innerHeight); e.preventDefault(); }, { passive:false });
    handle.addEventListener("touchend",   () => { if (startY===null) return; startY = null; snapNearest(); });
    handle.addEventListener("mousedown",  (e) => { startY = e.clientY; startSnap = curSnap();
      const mv = (ev) => applyLive(startSnap - (ev.clientY - startY)/window.innerHeight);
      const up = () => { document.removeEventListener("mousemove", mv); document.removeEventListener("mouseup", up); startY = null; snapNearest(); };
      document.addEventListener("mousemove", mv); document.addEventListener("mouseup", up); e.preventDefault(); });
  })();

  // ── badge text: HH:MM today, else MM/DD HH:MM ──
  function badgeText(ts) { const d = new Date(ts), n = new Date();
    const hh = String(d.getHours()).padStart(2,"0"), mm = String(d.getMinutes()).padStart(2,"0");
    return d.toDateString()===n.toDateString() ? `${hh}:${mm}`
      : `${String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,"0")}/${String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,"0")} ${hh}:${mm}`; }

  // ── one row: check → TIME BADGE → body → by → × ──
  function buildRow(it) {
    const row = document.createElement("div");
    row.className = "checklist-item" + (it.done ? " done" : "");
    const check = document.createElement("button");
    check.className = "checklist-check"; check.onclick = () => toggle(it); // API: POST /api/checklist/:id/toggle
    row.appendChild(check);
    if (it.trigger_at) { const t = document.createElement("span");
      t.className = "checklist-trigger"; t.textContent = badgeText(it.trigger_at); row.appendChild(t); }
    const body = document.createElement("div"); body.className = "checklist-body"; body.textContent = it.body; row.appendChild(body);
    if (it.trigger_at && it.trigger_at <= Date.now() && !it.done) {   // overdue → small "!"
      const bang = document.createElement("span"); bang.className = "checklist-overdue-bang"; bang.textContent = "!"; row.appendChild(bang);
    }
    if (it.created_by === "assistant") { const by = document.createElement("span");
      by.className = "checklist-by"; by.textContent = NAMES.assistant; row.appendChild(by); }
    const del = document.createElement("button");
    del.className = "checklist-del-x"; del.onclick = () => remove(it); // API: DELETE /api/checklist/:id
    row.appendChild(del);
    return row;
  }

  // ── render: overdue-but-not-done floats to top (marked with a small "!"); rest keeps order ──
  function render() {
    const list = $("list"); list.innerHTML = "";
    const t = Date.now();
    const overdue = ITEMS.filter(x => x.trigger_at && x.trigger_at <= t && !x.done);
    const rest    = ITEMS.filter(x => !(x.trigger_at && x.trigger_at <= t && !x.done));
    [...overdue, ...rest].forEach(it => list.appendChild(buildRow(it)));
    const nr = document.createElement("div");
    nr.className = "checklist-item checklist-item-new";
    nr.innerHTML = '<button class="checklist-check"></button><div class="checklist-body" contenteditable></div><button class="checklist-del-x"></button>';
    // API: on Enter -> POST /api/checklist { body, created_by:"user", is_fixed:1 }
    list.appendChild(nr);
    renderChrome();
  }

  function renderChrome() {
    const d = new Date();
    const y = d.getFullYear(), m = String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,"0"), day = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,"0");
    const wd = ["周日","周一","周二","周三","周四","周五","周六"][d.getDay()];
    const set = (id,v) => { const el=$(id); if (el) el.textContent = v; };
    set("rc-brand", `${APP_NAME} · GENERAL STORE`);
    set("rc-clerks", `${NAMES.assistant} · ${NAMES.user}`);
    set("rc-foot", `今天也辛苦了，${NAMES.user}。\n零点之后，未结清的会自动清零。`);
    set("rc-date", `${y} / ${m} / ${day} · ${wd}`);
    set("rc-now", `${String(d.getHours()).padStart(2,"0")}:${String(d.getMinutes()).padStart(2,"0")}`);
    const total = ITEMS.length, done = ITEMS.filter(x=>x.done).length, dayNum = String(total).padStart(3,"0");
    set("rc-serial", `#${String(y).slice(2)}-${m}-${day}-${dayNum}`);
    set("rc-total-label", String(total)); set("rc-subtotal", `${total} 项`);
    set("rc-done", `${done} 项`); set("rc-undone", `${total-done} 项`);
    const pct = total ? done/total : 0, w = 18, filled = Math.round(pct*w);
    set("rc-progress-bar", "█".repeat(filled) + "░".repeat(w-filled)); set("rc-progress-pct", `${Math.round(pct*100)}%`);
    const minsLeft = (24-d.getHours())*60 - d.getMinutes();
    set("rc-midnight", `${String(Math.floor(minsLeft/60)).padStart(2,"0")}:${String(minsLeft%60).padStart(2,"0")} 后`);
    const dash = "─ ".repeat(18).trim();
    ["rc-dashes-1","rc-dashes-2","rc-dashes-3","rc-dashes-4"].forEach(id => set(id, dash));
    const box = $("rc-barcode");
    if (box && !box.dataset.built) {
      const seed = [1,2,1,3,1,2,2,1,3,1,1,2,1,3,2,1,1,2,1,3,1,2,2,1,3,2,1,1,2,1,3,1,1,2,1,2];
      box.innerHTML = seed.map((wpx,i)=>`<i style="width:${wpx}px;height:${i%5===0?38:32}px"></i>`).join("");
      box.dataset.built = "1";
    }
    set("rc-barcode-serial", `${String(y).slice(2)} ${m} ${day} ${dayNum}`);
  }

  // ── actions (local-only; swap bodies for real fetch calls) ──
  async function toggle(it) { it.done = it.done ? 0 : 1; render();
    /* API: await fetch(`/api/checklist/${it.id}/toggle`, { method:"POST" }); */ }
  async function remove(it) { ITEMS = ITEMS.filter(x => x.id !== it.id); render();
    /* API: await fetch(`/api/checklist/${it.id}`, { method:"DELETE" }); */ }

  // On a real client: `ITEMS = (await (await fetch("/api/checklist")).json()).items;`
  // Auto-open once so the demo shows the drawer; drag the handle to resize.
  render();
  setTimeout(openSheet, 250);
</script>
</body>
</html>
